response.justification: While Bogdan possesses strong technical skills and extensive architecture experience, he is dramatically overqualified for an entry-level junior developer position, creating significant retention risks and expectations misalignment. More critically, his German language proficiency is only 'Basic,' falling substantially short of the required 'strong verbal and written communication in German' explicitly needed for client-facing consulting work in Germany's insurance sector. The role's junior scope and compensation would likely not retain an engineer of this caliber.
strengths: Extensive software architecture and design expertise across multiple complex systems and tech stacks
Proven proficiency in Java and object-oriented programming demonstrated across numerous professional roles
Strong track record with agile methodologies and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pair programming)
concerns: Dramatically overqualified—staff/senior-level experience at major companies suggests misalignment with entry-level expectations and rapid departure risk
German language proficiency is only 'Basic' despite explicit requirement for 'strong verbal and written communication in German' for client-facing consulting
No demonstrated experience in insurance sector or client-facing IT consulting—background is primarily product development
